title: SoftBank is reportedly weighing a deal for Swiss robotics startup Gravis
excerpt: Gravis Robotics, an ETH Zurich spinout, fits autonomy kits to excavators. A tie-up would extend Masayoshi Son’s robotics buying spree from the factory floor to the building site. SoftBank is weighing a deal for Gravis Robotics, a Swiss startup that fits excavators and diggers with the sensors and software to run themselves, according to Bloomberg. The […]
content_html:
Gravis Robotics, an ETH Zurich spinout, fits autonomy kits to excavators. A tie-up would extend Masayoshi Son’s robotics buying spree from the factory floor to the building site.

SoftBank is weighing a deal for Gravis Robotics, a Swiss startup that fits excavators and diggers with the sensors and software to run themselves, according to Bloomberg.

The report did not spell out the shape of any transaction, and the size, structure, and valuation were not disclosed. The discussions appear to be at an early stage, and there is no guarantee they lead anywhere.

What they do signal is where SoftBank is looking next. Masayoshi Son’s group has spent the past year assembling a robotics portfolio at speed, from an $800mn round it is reportedly circling for Agile Robots to smaller bets on the kind of autonomy kits that bolt onto existing machinery.

The 💜 of EU tech

The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

Gravis Robotics spun out of ETH Zurich in 2022, from the Swiss university’s Robotic Systems Lab. Rather than build a robot from scratch, it retrofits standard heavy equipment, attaching a kit that fuses LiDAR, cameras, GNSS positioning, and hydraulic sensors so an ordinary excavator can trench, grade soil, and manage stockpiles on its own.

The company says the approach can lift site output by roughly 30%.

The kit has a name, or two. The hardware, Gravis calls Rack; the operator controls it through a tablet interface named Slate that switches between autonomous and manual modes.

The pitch is that a machine can be taught to feel the soil through its hydraulics rather than follow a fixed programme.

It is run by chief executive Ryan Luke Johns, an architect turned roboticist, alongside chief technology officer Dominic Jud and co-founder Marco Hutter, an ETH robotics professor.

Johns and Jud hold a Guinness World Record together for the largest robot-built dry-stone wall, which tells you something about the company’s temperament.

In November 2025 the startup raised $23mn in a round co-led by IQ Capital and Zacua Ventures, with Pear VC, Sunna Ventures, and cement group Holcim among the backers.

By its own account, Gravis now has machines working on active sites across seven countries. Its customer list, per the company’s own announcements, includes contractor Taylor Woodrow, which used the technology at Manchester Airport, plant-hire firm Flannery, and Holcim, which runs it in quarries. Those claims come from Gravis and have not been independently audited.

For SoftBank, construction is a logical extension of a much larger thesis. The group agreed last October to buy ABB’s robotics division for $5.4bn, and a four-bank syndicate only finished putting together the roughly $1.75bn loan behind that purchase this week.

It is also selling its remaining stake in Boston Dynamics to Hyundai, tidying up an older robotics bet even as it places newer ones.

The Gravis talks, if real, would join a run of similar moves. SoftBank led Agile Robots’ 2021 financing, the round that made the Munich firm Germany’s first robotics unicorn, and is now said to be anchoring a fresh $800mn raise there.

Earlier in 2025 it put $500mn into Skild AI, a startup building a general-purpose model to control robots. The common thread is machines that do physical work, and software good enough to run them.

Son’s pitch is that artificial intelligence has matured in software and the next frontier is giving it a body. SoftBank is reportedly preparing a US-based AI and robotics vehicle, provisionally called Roze, that it hopes to float at a $100bn valuation.

The wider market has moved with him, with global robotics investment more than doubling to $27.6bn in 2025, the year of record rounds for firms such as NEURA Robotics.

A digger that drives itself is a less photogenic proposition than a walking humanoid, but it may be a more immediately useful one. Construction faces a persistent shortage of skilled machine operators, and earthmoving is repetitive, dangerous, and expensive to staff.

Whether SoftBank ends up buying into Gravis, or simply kicking the tyres, the direction of travel is clear enough. The company that once bet on a chatty humanoid named Pepper now wants the machines that move actual earth.

sentences.en: For SoftBank, construction is a logical extension of a much larger thesis.
sentences.zh: 对于软银来说，构建是一个更大论文的逻辑延伸。

sentences.en: The group agreed last October to buy ABB’s robotics division for $5.4bn, and a four-bank syndicate only finished putting together the roughly $1.75bn loan behind that purchase this week.
sentences.zh: 该集团去年 10 月同意以 54 亿美元收购 ABB 的机器人部门，而由四家银行组成的财团本周才完成了这笔收购背后的约 17.5 亿美元贷款。

blocks.html_en: It is also selling its remaining stake in Boston Dynamics to Hyundai, tidying up an older robotics bet even as it places newer ones.

sentences.en: It is also selling its remaining stake in Boston Dynamics to Hyundai, tidying up an older robotics bet even as it places newer ones.
sentences.zh: 它还将其在波士顿动力公司的剩余股份出售给现代汽车，在押注新机器人的同时，也清理了旧的机器人赌注。

blocks.html_en: The Gravis talks, if real, would join a run of similar moves. SoftBank led Agile Robots’ 2021 financing, the round that made the Munich firm Germany’s first robotics unicorn, and is now said to be anchoring a fresh $800mn raise there.

sentences.en: The Gravis talks, if real, would join a run of similar moves.
sentences.zh: 格雷维斯会谈如果成真，将会加入一系列类似的行动。

sentences.en: SoftBank led Agile Robots’ 2021 financing, the round that made the Munich firm Germany’s first robotics unicorn, and is now said to be anchoring a fresh $800mn raise there.
sentences.zh: 软银领投了 Agile Robots 的 2021 年融资，该轮融资使这家慕尼黑公司成为德国第一家机器人独角兽公司，据称目前该公司正在筹集 8 亿美元的新资金。

blocks.html_en: Earlier in 2025 it put $500mn into Skild AI, a startup building a general-purpose model to control robots. The common thread is machines that do physical work, and software good enough to run them.

sentences.en: Earlier in 2025 it put $500mn into Skild AI, a startup building a general-purpose model to control robots.
sentences.zh: 2025 年初，它向 Skild AI 投资了 5 亿美元，这是一家构建控制机器人的通用模型的初创公司。

sentences.en: The common thread is machines that do physical work, and software good enough to run them.
sentences.zh: 共同点是执行物理工作的机器以及足以运行它们的软件。

blocks.html_en: Son’s pitch is that artificial intelligence has matured in software and the next frontier is giving it a body. SoftBank is reportedly preparing a US-based AI and robotics vehicle, provisionally called Roze, that it hopes to float at a $100bn valuation.

sentences.en: Son’s pitch is that artificial intelligence has matured in software and the next frontier is giving it a body.
sentences.zh: 孙正义的主张是，人工智能在软件方面已经成熟，下一个前沿领域是赋予它实体。

sentences.en: SoftBank is reportedly preparing a US-based AI and robotics vehicle, provisionally called Roze, that it hopes to float at a $100bn valuation.
sentences.zh: 据报道，软银正在准备一款总部位于美国的人工智能和机器人汽车，暂定名为 Roze，希望以 1000 亿美元的估值上市。

blocks.html_en: The wider market has moved with him, with global robotics investment more than doubling to $27.6bn in 2025, the year of record rounds for firms such as NEURA Robotics.

sentences.en: The wider market has moved with him, with global robotics investment more than doubling to $27.6bn in 2025, the year of record rounds for firms such as NEURA Robotics.
sentences.zh: 更广泛的市场也随着他的脚步而变化，到 2025 年，全球机器人投资将增加一倍多，达到 276 亿美元，这一年 NEURA Robotics 等公司的融资金额将创下纪录。

blocks.html_en: A digger that drives itself is a less photogenic proposition than a walking humanoid, but it may be a more immediately useful one. Construction faces a persistent shortage of skilled machine operators, and earthmoving is repetitive, dangerous, and expensive to staff.

sentences.en: A digger that drives itself is a less photogenic proposition than a walking humanoid, but it may be a more immediately useful one.
sentences.zh: 与行走的人形机器人相比，自动驾驶的挖掘机是一个不太上镜的提议，但它可能是一个更直接有用的提议。

sentences.en: Construction faces a persistent shortage of skilled machine operators, and earthmoving is repetitive, dangerous, and expensive to staff.
sentences.zh: 建筑施工面临着熟练机械操作员持续短缺的问题，而土方搬运工作重复、危险且对人员来说成本高昂。

blocks.html_en: Whether SoftBank ends up buying into Gravis, or simply kicking the tyres, the direction of travel is clear enough. The company that once bet on a chatty humanoid named Pepper now wants the machines that move actual earth.

sentences.en: Whether SoftBank ends up buying into Gravis, or simply kicking the tyres, the direction of travel is clear enough.
sentences.zh: 无论软银最终是收购 Gravis，还是只是尝试一下，前进的方向都很明确。

sentences.en: The company that once bet on a chatty humanoid named Pepper now wants the machines that move actual earth.
sentences.zh: 这家曾经把赌注押在一个名叫 Pepper 的健谈类人机器人身上，现在想要的是能够移动地球的机器。

content_html_zh:
Gravis Robotics 是苏黎世联邦理工学院的衍生公司，为挖掘机安装自主套件。 此次合作将使孙正义的机器人采购热潮从工厂车间延伸到建筑工地。

据彭博社报道，软银正在考虑收购瑞士初创公司 Gravis Robotics，该公司为挖掘机和挖掘机配备自动运行的传感器和软件。

该报告没有详细说明任何交易的形式，也没有披露规模、结构和估值。 讨论似乎还处于早期阶段，并不能保证它们会取得任何进展。

它们确实表明了软银下一步的发展方向。 Masayoshi Son 的团队在过去的一年里快速组建了机器人产品组合，从据报道正在为 Agile Robots 进行的 8 亿美元融资，到对现有机械上的自动化套件的较小赌注。

欧盟科技的💜

来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。 立即注册！

Gravis Robotics 于 2022 年从苏黎世联邦理工学院的瑞士大学机器人系统实验室分拆出来。 它不是从头开始建造机器人，而是对标准重型设备进行改造，附加一个融合了激光雷达、摄像头、GNSS 定位和液压传感器的套件，这样普通的挖掘机就可以自行开沟、平整土壤和管理库存。

该公司表示，这种方法可以将站点产出提高约 30%。

该套件有一个或两个名称。 Gravis 将硬件称为 Rack；操作员通过名为 Slate 的平板电脑界面对其进行控制，该界面可在自动模式和手动模式之间切换。

其要点是，可以教会机器通过其液压系统来感受土壤，而不是遵循固定的程序。

该公司由首席执行官 Ryan Luke Johns（一位建筑师出身的机器人专家）、首席技术官 Dominic Jud 和联合创始人 Marco Hutter（ETH 机器人学教授）共同运营。

约翰斯和贾德共同保持着最大的机器人建造干石墙的吉尼斯世界纪录，这足以说明该公司的气质。

2025 年 11 月，这家初创公司在 IQ Capital 和 Zacua Ventures 共同领投的一轮融资中筹集了 2300 万美元，支持者包括 Pear VC、Sunna Ventures 和水泥集团 Holcim。

根据 Gravis 自己的说法，目前，Gravis 的机器在七个国家的活跃站点上运行。 根据该公司自己的公告，其客户名单包括在曼彻斯特机场使用该技术的承包商 Taylor Woodrow、设备租赁公司 Flannery 和在采石场运营该技术的 Holcim。 这些说法来自 Gravis，未经独立审计。

对于软银来说，构建是一个更大论文的逻辑延伸。 该集团去年 10 月同意以 54 亿美元收购 ABB 的机器人部门，而由四家银行组成的财团本周才完成了这笔收购背后的约 17.5 亿美元贷款。

它还将其在波士顿动力公司的剩余股份出售给现代汽车，在押注新机器人的同时，也清理了旧的机器人赌注。

格雷维斯会谈如果成真，将会加入一系列类似的行动。 软银领投了 Agile Robots 的 2021 年融资，该轮融资使这家慕尼黑公司成为德国第一家机器人独角兽公司，据称目前该公司正在筹集 8 亿美元的新资金。

2025 年初，它向 Skild AI 投资了 5 亿美元，这是一家构建控制机器人的通用模型的初创公司。 共同点是执行物理工作的机器以及足以运行它们的软件。

孙正义的主张是，人工智能在软件方面已经成熟，下一个前沿领域是赋予它实体。 据报道，软银正在准备一款总部位于美国的人工智能和机器人汽车，暂定名为 Roze，希望以 1000 亿美元的估值上市。

更广泛的市场也随着他的脚步而变化，到 2025 年，全球机器人投资将增加一倍多，达到 276 亿美元，这一年 NEURA Robotics 等公司的融资金额将创下纪录。

与行走的人形机器人相比，自动驾驶的挖掘机是一个不太上镜的提议，但它可能是一个更直接有用的提议。 建筑施工面临着熟练机械操作员持续短缺的问题，而土方搬运工作重复、危险且对人员来说成本高昂。

无论软银最终是收购 Gravis，还是只是尝试一下，前进的方向都很明确。 这家曾经把赌注押在一个名叫 Pepper 的健谈类人机器人身上，现在想要的是能够移动地球的机器。
